methods.name: uk_geo_utils.helpers.PAFAddressFormatter.__init__
documentation: one to one mapping.

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.helpers.PAFAddressFormatter.generate_address_label
documentation:
Construct a list for address label.

Non-empty premises elements are appended to the address label in the
order of organisation_name, department_name, po_box_number (which
must be prepended with 'PO Box', sub_building_name, building_name,
building_number, then the rest of the elements except for Town and
Postcode because we want them in their own fields. This isn't strict
address label but we're probably loading them into a database.

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.helpers.PAFAddressFormatter._is_exception_rule
documentation:
Check for "exception rule".

Address elements will be appended onto a new line on the label except
for when the penultimate lable line fulfils certain criteria, in which
case the element will be concatenated onto the penultimate line. This
method checks for those criteria.

i) First and last characters of the Building Name are numeric
  (eg '1to1' or '100:1')
ii) First and penultimate characters are numeric, last character is
  alphabetic (eg '12A')
iii) Building Name has only one character (eg 'A')

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.helpers.PAFAddressFormatter._append_to_label
documentation:
Append address element to the label.

Normally an element will be appended onto the list, except where the
existing last element fulfils the exception rule, in which case the
element will be concatenated onto the final list member.

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.helpers.PAFAddressFormatter.__str__
documentation: Return the label form of the address.

modules.name: uk_geo_utils.tests.test_postcode_helper
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.tests.test_postcode_helper.PostcodeHelperTest.test_create_from_object
documentation:
We may create a Postcode object from either a string, or an existing
Postcode object. This is intentional and gives us a nice property,
because it means we can declare a function like:

```
def do_thing(postcode)
    a = Postcode(postcode).with_space
    bla
```

and we can call `do_thing('AA1 1AA')` with a string representation
of a postcode or `do_thing(postcode)` with an existing Postcode object
and it will transparently "just work"

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.tests.test_addressbase_geocoder.AddressBaseGeocoderTest.test_empty_addressbase_table
documentation: The AddressBase table has no records in it

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.tests.test_addressbase_geocoder.AddressBaseGeocoderTest.test_empty_onsud_table
documentation: The ONSUD table has no records in it

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.tests.test_addressbase_geocoder.AddressBaseGeocoderTest.test_no_records
documentation: We can't find any records for the given postcode in the AddressBase table

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.tests.test_addressbase_geocoder.AddressBaseGeocoderTest.test_no_codes
documentation:
We find records for the given postcode in the AddressBase table
but there are no corresponding records in the ONSUD for the UPRNs we found

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.tests.test_addressbase_geocoder.AddressBaseGeocoderTest.test_valid
documentation:
We find records for the given postcode in the AddressBase table
There are some corresponding records in the ONSUD for the UPRNs we found

Valid result should be returned

Note that in this case, the ONSUD table does not contain corresponding
records for *all* of the UPRNs we found, but we accept the result anyway

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.tests.test_addressbase_geocoder.AddressBaseGeocoderTest.test_strict_mode
documentation:
We find records for the given postcode in the AddressBase table
There are some corresponding records in the ONSUD for the UPRNs we found

Note that in this case, the ONSUD table does not contain corresponding
records for *all* of the UPRNs we found, and we are passing strict=True
so we raise a StrictMatchException

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.tests.test_addressbase_geocoder.AddressBaseGeocoderTest.test_multiple_codes
documentation:
We find records for the given postcode in the AddressBase table
There are corresponding records in the ONSUD for the UPRNs we found
The UPRNs described by this postcode map to more than one 'lad'
but they all map to the same 'cty'

documentation: valid get_code() by UPRN queries

classes.name: uk_geo_utils.management.commands.import_onspd.Command
documentation:
To import ONSPD, grab the latest release:
https://ons.maps.arcgis.com/home/search.html?t=content&q=ONS%20Postcode%20Directory
and run:
    python manage.py update_onspd --data-path /path/to/ONSPD_MAY_2024/Data

methods.name: uk_geo_utils.models.AbstractOnspd._get_cty

classes.name: uk_geo_utils.management.commands.import_onsud.Command
documentation:
To import ONSUD, grab the latest release:
http://ons.maps.arcgis.com/home/search.html?q=ONS%20Address%20Directory&t=content
and run
python manage.py import_onsud /path/to/data
